data.description:
# Sweep: CLI surface (_cli/, _commands/) — diagnostics to stderr, KEEP stdout data prints

The CLI surface is where stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` data output legitimately lives — that is a
machine contract and MUST stay on stdout (the reconciler `json.loads` it). Only migrate *diagnostic*
stderr prints. Most excepts here re-raise `CommandError` (low risk).

## What
- Audit prints in `src/rebar/_cli/` and `src/rebar/_commands/`: classify each as stdout DATA (keep as
  `print`) vs stderr DIAGNOSTIC (migrate to the named logger). Do not move data output off stdout.
- Bring broad-excepts in these packages to the observability floor (log `exc_info` or justified noqa).
- Confirm the T201 per-file-ignore for the CLI presentation layer is correct (data prints allowed here).
- Remove these packages from the BLE001 allowlist as they become conformant.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stdout data `print(json.dumps(...))` output in `_cli/`/`_commands/` is unchanged (contract intact).
- [ ] Diagnostic stderr prints migrate to the logger; broad-excepts meet the observability floor.
- [ ] The T201 per-file-ignore covers the CLI presentation layer; library/core/MCP stay print-free.
- [ ] These packages removed from the BLE001 allowlist; `make lint` + full test suite pass.
data.title: Sweep: CLI surface (_cli, _commands) — diagnostics to stderr, keep stdout data prints
